basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Al-Shūrā — “Consultation” — opens with the disconnected letters Ḥā-Mīm, ʿAyn-Sīn-Qāf, of which Qushayrī makes the keys of the divine Names: from the Hāʾ the Forbearing, from the Mīm the King, from the ʿAyn the Knowing, from the Sīn the Hearing, from the Qāf the Near. The sūra affirms that revelation descends upon Muḥammad as it descended upon the prophets before him: one Word from one Throne. The heavens are near to splitting in awe while the angels glorify their Lord and ask forgiveness for those on the earth. It paints the believers “whose affair is consultation among them” and who spend from what God gives them, and their Prophet who asks “no reward save love for kinship.” It proclaims absolute transcendence — “there is nothing like unto Him” —, the acceptance of repentance, the pardon that “excuses much.” And it culminates on the Name al-Laṭīf, “God is subtle toward His servants”: Qushayrī unfolds there one of his most beautiful pages, where of His subtlety toward the servant is that He makes him know that He is Subtle — for without His subtlety, none would have known that He is. It closes upon the Qurʾān-Spirit that revives the heart and upon the return of all things to God.
themes: the keys of the divine Names

versets.ar: حم
versets.tr: ḥā mīm
versets.trad: Ḥā. Mīm.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of these isolated letters the keys of the divine Names: the Hāʾ opens Ḥalīm (the Forbearing), Ḥāfiẓ (the Keeper) and Ḥakīm (the Wise); the Mīm opens Malik (the King), Mājid and Majīd (the Glorious), Mannān (the Bountiful), Muʾmin (the Giver of security) and Muhaymin (the Watchful). Thus the sūra opens upon the very atmosphere of the Names its verses evoke: the Mighty, the Wise, the Forgiving, the Compassionate.

mots.def: two opening letters, keys of the divine Names per Qushayrī.

versets.ar: عسق
versets.tr: ʿayn sīn qāf
versets.trad: ʿAyn. Sīn. Qāf.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī continues his deciphering: the ʿAyn opens ʿĀlim (the Knowing), ʿAdl (the Just) and ʿĀl (the Most High); the Sīn opens Sayyid (the Master), Samīʿ (the Hearing) and Sarīʿ al-ḥisāb (swift in reckoning); the Qāf opens Qādir, Qāhir (the Subduer), Qarīb (the Near), Qadīr and Quddūs (the All-Holy). The oath is thus sworn by the Names themselves, and the revelation that follows is its seal.

mots.def: three opening letters, keys of the divine Names per Qushayrī.

versets.ar: كَذلِكَ يُوحِي إِلَيْكَ وَإِلَى الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ اللَّهُ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: kadhālika yūḥī ilayka wa-ilā lladhīna min qablika llāhu l-ʿazīzu l-ḥakīmu
versets.trad: Thus does God, the Mighty, the Wise, send revelation to you as to those before you.
versets.commentaire: The oath borne by the Names and letters arrives here: just as He revealed to those before you, so does He reveal to you, the Mighty, the Wise. Qushayrī stresses the unity of the source: it is the same Inspirer who spoke to the earlier prophets and who speaks to Muḥammad. Revelation is no rupture but the continuity of one Word descending from the same Throne.

mots.racine: ع ز ز / ح ك م
mots.def: two divine Names sealing the verse.

versets.ar: لَهُ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ وَهُوَ الْعَلِيُّ الْعَظِيمُ
versets.tr: lahu mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi wa-huwa l-ʿaliyyu l-ʿaẓīmu
versets.trad: To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. He is the Sublime, the Magnificent.
versets.commentaire: To Him belong the heavens and the earth, in full ownership (mulk). Qushayrī clarifies that His loftiness and His greatness are not situated: they mean that He necessarily merits the attributes of majesty and glory. The Sublime (al-ʿalī) is not high by place but by rank; the Magnificent (al-ʿaẓīm) is not great by measure but by the absolute dignity of His Being.

mots.sens: to Him what is in the heavens

mots.racine: ع ل و / ع ظ م
mots.def: loftiness and greatness of rank, not of place.

versets.ar: تَكادُ السَّماواتُ يَتَفَطَّرْنَ مِنْ فَوْقِهِنَّ وَالْمَلائِكَةُ يُسَبِّحُونَ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّهِمْ وَيَسْتَغْفِرُونَ لِمَنْ فِي الْأَرْضِ أَلا إِنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ
versets.tr: takādu s-samāwātu yatafaṭṭarna min fawqihinna wa-l-malāʾikatu yusabbiḥūna bi-ḥamdi rabbihim wa-yastaghfirūna li-man fī l-arḍi alā inna llāha huwa l-ghafūru r-raḥīmu
versets.trad: The heavens are near to splitting apart from above, while the angels glorify their Lord with praise and ask forgiveness for those on the earth. Is not God indeed the Forgiving, the Compassionate?
versets.commentaire: The heavens nearly split before the grandeur of Him who is above them — splitting from the summit downward, says Qushayrī, out of excess of awe. Though the children of Adam are so rebellious, the angels never cease to glorify their Lord and to ask forgiveness for those on the earth. And here the striking contrast: at the very moment when the disbelievers commit the enormous crime of associating partners, He withholds from them neither their sustenance nor His bounty — even though He wills to chastise them in the hereafter.

mots.sens: they ask forgiveness for those on earth

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ اتَّخَذُوا مِنْ دُونِهِ أَوْلِياءَ اللَّهُ حَفِيظٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَما أَنْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ بِوَكِيلٍ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna ttakhadhū min dūnihi awliyāʾa llāhu ḥafīẓun ʿalayhim wa-mā anta ʿalayhim bi-wakīlin
versets.trad: Those who take protectors apart from Him — God watches over them; and you are not a guardian over them.
versets.commentaire: Those who take the devils as protectors, by yielding to their whisperings, do not escape God: He knows their affair and will chastise them as they deserve. And you, Muḥammad, are not appointed to compel them. Qushayrī widens it in an ishāra: whoever acts following his passion, transgresses a limit of God or breaks a covenant with Him, takes the devils as allies; God knows him and nothing of his affair is hidden from Him — then, if He wills, He chastises him, and if He wills, He forgives him.

mots.def: ittakhadha: to take for oneself, to adopt.

versets.ar: وَكَذلِكَ أَوْحَيْنا إِلَيْكَ قُرْآناً عَرَبِيًّا لِتُنْذِرَ أُمَّ الْقُرى وَمَنْ حَوْلَها وَتُنْذِرَ يَوْمَ الْجَمْعِ لا رَيْبَ فِيهِ فَرِيقٌ فِي الْجَنَّةِ وَفَرِيقٌ فِي السَّعِيرِ
versets.tr: wa-kadhālika awḥaynā ilayka qurʾānan ʿarabiyyan li-tundhira umma l-qurā wa-man ḥawlahā wa-tundhira yawma l-jamʿi lā rayba fīhi farīqun fī l-jannati wa-farīqun fī s-saʿīri
versets.trad: Thus have We revealed to you an Arabic Qurʾān, that you may warn the Mother of Cities and those around it, and warn of the Day of Gathering, of which there is no doubt. A party shall be in the Garden, and a party in the Blaze.
versets.commentaire: A Qurʾān recited in the tongue of the Arabs, to warn Mecca and what surrounds it — for Mecca is the navel of the earth, and the whole world circles around the Kaʿba. To warn above all of the Day of Gathering, when all creatures are assembled: the gathering of a person with his deed, of the body with its soul, of each with its like in good and in evil. Qushayrī draws the present sense: as they are already today two parties — one in the sweetness of obedience, the other in the darkness of denial — so will they be tomorrow: the people of the encounter, and the people of affliction.

mots.racine: أ م م / ق ر ي
mots.def: umm al-qurā: one of the names of Mecca.
the Day when all creatures are assembled.

versets.ar: وَلَوْ شاءَ اللَّهُ لَجَعَلَهُمْ أُمَّةً واحِدَةً وَلكِنْ يُدْخِلُ مَنْ يَشاءُ فِي رَحْمَتِهِ وَالظَّالِمُونَ ما لَهُمْ مِنْ وَلِيٍّ وَلا نَصِيرٍ
versets.tr: wa-law shāʾa llāhu la-jaʿalahum ummatan wāḥidatan wa-lākin yudkhilu man yashāʾu fī raḥmatihi wa-ẓ-ẓālimūna mā lahum min waliyyin wa-lā naṣīrin
versets.trad: Had God so willed, He would have made them one community; but He admits whom He wills into His mercy. As for the wrongdoers, they have neither protector nor helper.
versets.commentaire: Had He willed to gather them all upon guidance, no obstacle could have stopped Him; and had He willed to gather them all upon error, none could have turned Him aside. Qushayrī: having created them diverse — as He willed — He has no concern for their objections: He is one God, sovereign, whom none commands, manager of all affairs, of good and evil, of profit and harm. Outside His mercy, the wrongdoer finds neither ally nor help.

mots.racine: أ م م / و ح د
و ل ي / ن ص ر

versets.ar: أَمِ اتَّخَذُوا مِنْ دُونِهِ أَوْلِياءَ فَاللَّهُ هُوَ الْوَلِيُّ وَهُوَ يُحْيِ الْمَوْتى وَهُوَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: ami ttakhadhū min dūnihi awliyāʾa fa-llāhu huwa l-waliyyu wa-huwa yuḥyi l-mawtā wa-huwa ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīrun
versets.trad: Or have they taken protectors apart from Him? Yet God alone is the Protector; He gives life to the dead, and He is powerful over all things.
versets.commentaire: He alone is the true Protector (al-walī). Qushayrī unfolds the inner sense of the giving of life: it is He who gives life to souls and hearts today and tomorrow, and who brings death to souls and hearts today and tomorrow. To revive the dead is thus not only the miracle of the Last Day: it is also, here below, the life God breathes into the heart that turns to Him and the death that seizes the heart turned away. He is powerful over all things, with none to thwart Him.

mots.def: al-walī: the ally, the sole guardian.

mots.racine: ح ي ي / م و ت
mots.sens: He gives life to the dead
mots.def: aḥyā: to revive; mawtā: the dead.

versets.ar: وَمَا اخْتَلَفْتُمْ فِيهِ مِنْ شَيْءٍ فَحُكْمُهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ ذلِكُمُ اللَّهُ رَبِّي عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ وَإِلَيْهِ أُنِيبُ
versets.tr: wa-mā khtalaftum fīhi min shayʾin fa-ḥukmuhu ilā llāhi dhālikumu llāhu rabbī ʿalayhi tawakkaltu wa-ilayhi unību
versets.trad: Whatever you differ about, its judgment belongs to God. Such is God, my Lord! In Him I trust, and to Him I turn in repentance.
versets.commentaire: Judgment belongs to God — that is, says Qushayrī, to the Book of God, the Sunna of His Prophet, the consensus of the imams and the indications of analogy: such is the law of the Way. Then he opens the ishāra: if you find no clarity and your thoughts contradict one another, abandon your own contriving, take refuge in the shade of contemplating His decree, and await what it befits you to do according to what He makes easy for you. And again: when your hearts are absorbed in concern for yourselves, not knowing whether your lot was sealed in felicity or in distress, hand the matter over to God and occupy the moment with His command, without speculating on what you have no path to know.

mots.sens: to Him I turn in repentance
mots.def: anāba: to turn back to God.

versets.ar: فاطِرُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ جَعَلَ لَكُمْ مِنْ أَنْفُسِكُمْ أَزْواجاً وَمِنَ الْأَنْعامِ أَزْواجاً يَذْرَؤُكُمْ فِيهِ لَيْسَ كَمِثْلِهِ شَيْءٌ وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْبَصِيرُ
versets.tr: fāṭiru s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi jaʿala lakum min anfusikum azwājan wa-mina l-anʿāmi azwājan yadhraʾukum fīhi laysa ka-mithlihi shayʾun wa-huwa s-samīʿu l-baṣīru
versets.trad: The Originator of the heavens and the earth. He has made for you mates from among yourselves, and mates among the cattle. By this He multiplies you. There is nothing like unto Him! He is the All-Hearing, the All-Seeing.
versets.commentaire: “There is nothing like unto Him”: Qushayrī dwells at length on this cardinal verse of transcendence. He has no equal that matches Him, no form that resembles Him; the Real has no like in His essence, nor in His attributes, nor in His acts. He then denounces three errors: those who liken His essence to that of creatures, ascribing to Him limit, term and place; worse still, those who attribute to Him organs — imagining His sight in a pupil, His hearing in a member, His power in a hand; and those who measure His decree by the yardstick of human judgment. All are people of likening (tashbīh), whereas the Real merits pure declaration of transcendence (tanzīh), oneness without delimitation.

mots.def: fāṭir: He who cleaves and brings forth, the Creator.

mots.sens: there is nothing like unto Him

versets.ar: لَهُ مَقالِيدُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ يَبْسُطُ الرِّزْقَ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ وَيَقْدِرُ إِنَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: lahu maqālīdu s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi yabsuṭu r-rizqa li-man yashāʾu wa-yaqdiru innahu bi-kulli shayʾin ʿalīmun
versets.trad: To Him belong the keys of the heavens and the earth; He extends provision to whom He wills, and measures it out. Indeed, He is Knowing of all things.
versets.commentaire: The keys (maqālīd) open His treasuries, and His treasuries are the expanse of His power. Qushayrī transposes provision to the inner plane: just as in the world there are diverse minerals, hearts too are mines of varied states' jewels: one heart is a mine of gnosis (maʿrifa), another of love, another of yearning, another of intimacy — not to mention unification, awe, contentment. Knowing that the keys are His bears a fruit: that the servant turn his thoughts away from creatures and toward Him who extends and restricts the provision of souls and hearts according to His will, His decree and His knowledge.

mots.racine: ب س ط / ر ز ق

versets.ar: شَرَعَ لَكُمْ مِنَ الدِّينِ ما وَصَّى بِهِ نُوحاً وَالَّذِي أَوْحَيْنا إِلَيْكَ وَما وَصَّيْنا بِهِ إِبْراهِيمَ وَمُوسى وَعِيسى أَنْ أَقِيمُوا الدِّينَ وَلا تَتَفَرَّقُوا فِيهِ كَبُرَ عَلَى الْمُشْرِكِينَ ما تَدْعُوهُمْ إِلَيْهِ اللَّهُ يَجْتَبِي إِلَيْهِ مَنْ يَشاءُ وَيَهْدِي إِلَيْهِ مَنْ يُنِيبُ
versets.tr: sharaʿa lakum mina d-dīni mā waṣṣā bihi nūḥan wa-lladhī awḥaynā ilayka wa-mā waṣṣaynā bihi ibrāhīma wa-mūsā wa-ʿīsā an aqīmū d-dīna wa-lā tatafarraqū fīhi kabura ʿalā l-mushrikīna mā tadʿūhum ilayhi llāhu yajtabī ilayhi man yashāʾu wa-yahdī ilayhi man yunību
versets.trad: He has ordained for you of religion what He enjoined upon Noah, and what We have revealed to you, and what We enjoined upon Abraham, Moses and Jesus: that you uphold the religion and do not divide therein. Grievous to the idolaters is that to which you call them. God draws to Himself whom He wills, and guides to Himself whoever turns in repentance.
versets.commentaire: “He has ordained” (sharaʿa): He set forth and made manifest. Qushayrī specifies that what is meant here are the foundations of religion (uṣūl al-dīn), for they differ in none of the revealed Laws; it is the branches (furūʿ) that vary. The verse thus designates those truths whose ruling is one in all the Laws: to uphold the religion and not divide therein. And the verse's close traces the twofold movement: God draws to Himself whom He wills by pure election, and guides to Himself whoever turns back.

mots.def: sharaʿa: to trace a path, to prescribe.

versets.ar: وَما تَفَرَّقُوا إِلاَّ مِنْ بَعْدِ ما جاءَهُمُ الْعِلْمُ بَغْياً بَيْنَهُمْ وَلَوْلا كَلِمَةٌ سَبَقَتْ مِنْ رَبِّكَ إِلى أَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى لَقُضِيَ بَيْنَهُمْ وَإِنَّ الَّذِينَ أُورِثُوا الْكِتابَ مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ لَفِي شَكٍّ مِنْهُ مُرِيبٍ
versets.tr: wa-mā tafarraqū illā min baʿdi mā jāʾahumu l-ʿilmu baghyan baynahum wa-law-lā kalimatun sabaqat min rabbika ilā ajalin musamman la-quḍiya baynahum wa-inna lladhīna ūrithū l-kitāba min baʿdihim la-fī shakkin minhu murībin
versets.trad: They divided only after knowledge had come to them, out of rivalry among themselves. Had not a word from your Lord already gone forth to an appointed term, it would have been decided between them. Those who inherited the Book after them are in disquieting doubt about it.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī explains it soberly: they persisted in their falsehood after the evidence of exposition and the manifesting of proof, when no excuse and no doubt remained. Their division came not from ignorance but from mutual rebellion (baghy), once knowledge had come. And the “word that precedes” from your Lord is His decree to defer the chastisement until the Day of resurrection; without it, what they demand would have been hastened to them. The reprieve is thus a mercy veiled in respite.

mots.racine: ك ل م / س ب ق

versets.ar: فَلِذلِكَ فَادْعُ وَاسْتَقِمْ كَما أُمِرْتَ وَلا تَتَّبِعْ أَهْواءَهُمْ وَقُلْ آمَنْتُ بِما أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ مِنْ كِتابٍ وَأُمِرْتُ لِأَعْدِلَ بَيْنَكُمُ اللَّهُ رَبُّنا وَرَبُّكُمْ لَنا أَعْمالُنا وَلَكُمْ أَعْمالُكُمْ لا حُجَّةَ بَيْنَنا وَبَيْنَكُمُ اللَّهُ يَجْمَعُ بَيْنَنا وَإِلَيْهِ الْمَصِيرُ
versets.tr: fa-li-dhālika fa-dʿu wa-staqim kamā umirta wa-lā tattabiʿ ahwāʾahum wa-qul āmantu bi-mā anzala llāhu min kitābin wa-umirtu li-aʿdila baynakumu llāhu rabbunā wa-rabbukum lanā aʿmālunā wa-lakum aʿmālukum lā ḥujjata baynanā wa-baynakumu llāhu yajmaʿu baynanā wa-ilayhi l-maṣīru
versets.trad: So call to the faith, and stand firm as you are commanded; do not follow their passions, and say: “I believe in whatever Book God has sent down. I am commanded to deal justly among you. God is our Lord and your Lord! To us our deeds and to you yours. There is no dispute between us and you. God will gather us together, and to Him is the final return.”
versets.commentaire: Call to this Qurʾān and to the pure religion; stand straight in the call and in obedience. Qushayrī notes a subtlety of language: the letters alif, sīn and tāʾ in the word istiqāma (“stand straight”) carry the sense of request and desire — as if it were said: “Ask Me to set you straight.” Rectitude is thus no solitary effort but a request addressed to Him who alone makes firm. As for the justice commanded, it is to know that God is the God of all, that He will tomorrow reckon each according to his deed, that the proof belongs to God over His creatures, and that it is they who have need of their Master.

mots.def: istaqāma: to hold firm in rectitude.

mots.sens: that I deal justly among you

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ يُحَاجُّونَ فِي اللَّهِ مِنْ بَعْدِ ما اسْتُجِيبَ لَهُ حُجَّتُهُمْ داحِضَةٌ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ وَعَلَيْهِمْ غَضَبٌ وَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ شَدِيدٌ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna yuḥājjūna fī llāhi min baʿdi mā stujība lahu ḥujjatuhum dāḥiḍatun ʿinda rabbihim wa-ʿalayhim ghaḍabun wa-lahum ʿadhābun shadīdun
versets.trad: Those who dispute concerning God after His call has been answered — their argument is void before their Lord; upon them is wrath, and for them a severe punishment.
versets.commentaire: They dispute concerning God after the Prophet's prayer was answered — an allusion, according to Qushayrī, to the day of Badr. The argument of these deniers is annulled (dāḥiḍa) before their Lord, for they arm themselves with falsehood; they earn from God the curse and the chastisement. What is called “argument” has but the name: in reality it is mere vain illusion, weightless in the balance of Truth once it has been recognized.

versets.ar: اللَّهُ الَّذِي أَنْزَلَ الْكِتابَ بِالْحَقِّ وَالْمِيزانَ وَما يُدْرِيكَ لَعَلَّ السَّاعَةَ قَرِيبٌ
versets.tr: allāhu lladhī anzala l-kitāba bi-l-ḥaqqi wa-l-mīzāna wa-mā yudrīka laʿalla s-sāʿata qarībun
versets.trad: It is God who sent down the Book with the Truth, and the Balance. And what will make you know? Perhaps the Hour is near.
versets.commentaire: He sent down the Book and, with it, judgment by the Balance — that is, says Qushayrī, by the Truth (al-ḥaqq). And he adds an inner reading: He inspired them to weigh things in the balance and to observe justice in all their states. As for “what will make you know? Perhaps the Hour is near,” Qushayrī sees in it a warning: it turns them away from long hope and rouses them to await the sudden assault of the term.

versets.ar: يَسْتَعْجِلُ بِهَا الَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِها وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مُشْفِقُونَ مِنْها وَيَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّهَا الْحَقُّ أَلا إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُمارُونَ فِي السَّاعَةِ لَفِي ضَلالٍ بَعِيدٍ
versets.tr: yastaʿjilu bihā lladhīna lā yuʾminūna bihā wa-lladhīna āmanū mushfiqūna minhā wa-yaʿlamūna annahā l-ḥaqqu alā inna lladhīna yumārūna fī s-sāʿati la-fī ḍalālin baʿīdin
versets.trad: Those who do not believe in it seek to hasten it, while the believers are in awe of it and know that it is the Truth. Are not those who dispute about the Hour in far-reaching error?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī paints the contrast of the two dispositions. The believers believe in the resurrection and what follows; they entrust their affairs to God, do not wish for death out of fear of trial, but when it comes do not hate it and are prepared for it. And he gives its sweetest reason: for death brings them near to the encounter — the encounter of the Beloved. The denier presses the Hour in defiance; the believer dreads it yet welcomes it, for it is the door to the beloved.

mots.def: mushfiq: one who dreads with reverence.
mārā: to wrangle, to doubt stubbornly.

versets.ar: اللَّهُ لَطِيفٌ بِعِبادِهِ يَرْزُقُ مَنْ يَشاءُ وَهُوَ الْقَوِيُّ الْعَزِيزُ
versets.tr: allāhu laṭīfun bi-ʿibādihi yarzuqu man yashāʾu wa-huwa l-qawiyyu l-ʿazīzu
versets.trad: God is subtle toward His servants; He provides for whom He wills; He is the Strong, the Mighty!
versets.commentaire: On the Name al-Laṭīf, Qushayrī opens one of his most beautiful developments. The Subtle is He who knows the finest and most hidden delicacies of things; and He is also He who lavishes the good — both senses suit His description. He then multiplies its faces: of His subtlety toward the servant is that He gives him beyond the sufficient and burdens him only below his strength; of His subtlety is that He veils from him the issue of his destiny, for if he knew his felicity he would lean on it and slacken his work, and if he knew his distress he would despair and abandon it; of His subtlety is that He hides from him the term of his life, lest he take fright should the hour draw near; of His subtlety is that He makes him forget the faults committed here below, so that life in the Garden be not spoiled for him. And this summit: of His subtlety toward the servant is that He makes him know that He is Subtle — for without His subtlety, none would have known that He is the Subtle.

mots.def: laṭīf: knowing the most subtle things; benevolent.

mots.racine: ق و ي / ع ز ز
mots.def: two Names of strength and might.

versets.ar: مَنْ كانَ يُرِيدُ حَرْثَ الْآخِرَةِ نَزِدْ لَهُ فِي حَرْثِهِ وَمَنْ كانَ يُرِيدُ حَرْثَ الدُّنْيا نُؤْتِهِ مِنْها وَما لَهُ فِي الْآخِرَةِ مِنْ نَصِيبٍ
versets.tr: man kāna yurīdu ḥartha l-ākhirati nazid lahu fī ḥarthihi wa-man kāna yurīdu ḥartha d-dunyā nuʾtihi minhā wa-mā lahu fī l-ākhirati min naṣībin
versets.trad: Whoever desires the harvest of the hereafter, We increase for him his harvest; and whoever desires the harvest of this world, We give him some of it, but he shall have no share in the hereafter.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī magnificently unfolds the increase promised to whoever desires the harvest of the hereafter: We increase it for him already today — in support in acts of obedience, in gnosis and in purity of states by way of realization; and We increase it for him in the hereafter in reward, in nearness, in forms of salvation and in manifold degrees. But to whoever aims only at the crop of this world, We give him what he wants, with no share for him in the hereafter. The intention is the seed: by the field one sows, the eternal harvest is decided.

mots.racine: ح ر ث / أ خ ر

versets.ar: أَمْ لَهُمْ شُرَكاءُ شَرَعُوا لَهُمْ مِنَ الدِّينِ ما لَمْ يَأْذَنْ بِهِ اللَّهُ وَلَوْلا كَلِمَةُ الْفَصْلِ لَقُضِيَ بَيْنَهُمْ وَإِنَّ الظَّالِمِينَ لَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: am lahum shurakāʾu sharaʿū lahum mina d-dīni mā lam yaʾdhan bihi llāhu wa-law-lā kalimatu l-faṣli la-quḍiya baynahum wa-inna ẓ-ẓālimīna lahum ʿadhābun alīmun
versets.trad: Or do they have partners who have ordained for them of religion what God has not sanctioned? Had it not been for the word of decision, it would have been settled between them. Indeed, for the wrongdoers is a painful punishment.
versets.commentaire: What God has not permitted — says Qushayrī — is nothing He commanded: it is but a fabrication on their part. They gave themselves partners who would have legislated for them a religion without God's leave. And “the word of separation” (kalimat al-faṣl) is the prior decree to defer chastisement until the Day of resurrection: without it, their affair would have been settled at once. The reprieve does not absolve; it only defers the pain reserved for the wrongdoers.

mots.def: kalimat al-faṣl: decree of separation, verdict.

versets.ar: تَرَى الظَّالِمِينَ مُشْفِقِينَ مِمَّا كَسَبُوا وَهُوَ واقِعٌ بِهِمْ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ فِي رَوْضاتِ الْجَنَّاتِ لَهُمْ ما يَشاؤُنَ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ ذلِكَ هُوَ الْفَضْلُ الْكَبِيرُ
versets.tr: tarā ẓ-ẓālimīna mushfiqīna mimmā kasabū wa-huwa wāqiʿun bihim wa-lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti fī rawḍāti l-jannāti lahum mā yashāʾūna ʿinda rabbihim dhālika huwa l-faḍlu l-kabīru
versets.trad: You will see the wrongdoers in dread of what they have earned, and it will befall them. As for those who believed and did righteous deeds, they shall be in the flowering meadows of the Gardens. They shall have whatever they wish with their Lord. That is the great favor!
versets.commentaire: To the believers, the meadows of the Gardens — and Qushayrī doubles them with a present and a future sense: here below, the gardens of union, the sweetness of obedience and worship, the fragrance of intimacy in the hours of seclusion; in the hereafter, the meadows of Paradise. “They shall have whatever they wish with their Lord” then becomes an ocean: if they desire the permanence of the divine subtlety, it will be permanent for them; if they desire the fullness of unveiling, it will be theirs. This is the great favor — not a finite good, but the very fulfillment of desire before the Face.

mots.racine: ر و ض / ج ن ن
ف ض ل / ك ب ر

versets.ar: ذلِكَ الَّذِي يُبَشِّرُ اللَّهُ عِبادَهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ قُلْ لا أَسْئَلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ أَجْراً إِلاَّ الْمَوَدَّةَ فِي الْقُرْبى وَمَنْ يَقْتَرِفْ حَسَنَةً نَزِدْ لَهُ فِيها حُسْناً إِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ شَكُورٌ
versets.tr: dhālika lladhī yubashshiru llāhu ʿibādahu lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti qul lā asʾalukum ʿalayhi ajran illā l-mawaddata fī l-qurbā wa-man yaqtarif ḥasanatan nazid lahu fīhā ḥusnan inna llāha ghafūrun shakūrun
versets.trad: That is the good tidings God gives to His servants who believe and do righteous deeds. Say: “I ask of you no reward for it, except love for kinship.” Whoever acquires a good deed, We increase for him its good. Indeed, God is Forgiving, Grateful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī marvels at the absolute disinterestedness: whoever brings another good tidings ordinarily claims a reward; but God, having announced to the believers through the tongue of His Prophet the eternal honors, claimed no reward — and His Prophet too asks no price. The “love for kinship” becomes for him love of all who draw near to God: love whoever draws near to God through His obedience. And on the increase of good he gives a rising gradation: this increase is the bestowal of subtlety after striving, the pure gift beyond all proportion — a surplus the servant cannot attain by his own capacity, beyond the reach of human power.

mots.racine: و د د / ق ر ب
ق ر ف / ح س ن

versets.ar: أَمْ يَقُولُونَ افْتَرى عَلَى اللَّهِ كَذِباً فَإِنْ يَشَإِ اللَّهُ يَخْتِمْ عَلى قَلْبِكَ وَيَمْحُ اللَّهُ الْباطِلَ وَيُحِقُّ الْحَقَّ بِكَلِماتِهِ إِنَّهُ عَلِيمٌ بِذاتِ الصُّدُورِ
versets.tr: am yaqūlūna ftarā ʿalā llāhi kadhiban fa-in yashaʾi llāhu yakhtim ʿalā qalbika wa-yamḥu llāhu l-bāṭila wa-yuḥiqqu l-ḥaqqa bi-kalimātihi innahu ʿalīmun bi-dhāti ṣ-ṣudūri
versets.trad: Or do they say: “He has forged a lie against God”? Yet if God willed, He would set a seal upon your heart. God effaces falsehood and confirms the Truth by His words. Indeed, He knows what the breasts conceal.
versets.commentaire: “Had you forged it, God would have sealed your heart — but you did not lie against your Lord.” Qushayrī draws the broad sense: God disposes of His servants as He wills: distancing and drawing near, intimacy and removal. The seal, the effacing of the false and the confirming of the true “by His words” are so many acts of this free sovereignty; and the final word — “He knows what the breasts contain” — closes the verse on the absolute intimacy of His knowledge.

mots.racine: ف ر ي / ك ذ ب
mots.sens: to forge a lie against God

versets.ar: وَهُوَ الَّذِي يَقْبَلُ التَّوْبَةَ عَنْ عِبادِهِ وَيَعْفُوا عَنِ السَّيِّئاتِ وَيَعْلَمُ ما تَفْعَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-huwa lladhī yaqbalu t-tawbata ʿan ʿibādihi wa-yaʿfū ʿani s-sayyiʾāti wa-yaʿlamu mā tafʿalūna
versets.trad: It is He who accepts repentance from His servants, pardons evil deeds, and knows what you do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse a praise of God by Himself: He has made Himself known to His servants in the mode of His own praise, saying that He accepts repentance: for the fault, even if it binds to the servant an attribute of blame, its acceptance binds to the Real a Name of praise. And he notes the delicacy of the word “His servants” (ʿibādih): this name calls for election, for He has related it to Himself — so much so that many masters wished to be reckoned among the first and the last, in the hope He might say to them: “My servant!”

mots.racine: ق ب ل / ت و ب
mots.def: qabila: to accept; tawba: repentant return.

versets.ar: وَيَسْتَجِيبُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ وَيَزِيدُهُمْ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ وَالْكافِرُونَ لَهُمْ عَذابٌ شَدِيدٌ
versets.tr: wa-yastajību lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti wa-yazīduhum min faḍlihi wa-l-kāfirūna lahum ʿadhābun shadīdun
versets.trad: He answers those who believe and do righteous deeds, and increases for them of His favor. As for the disbelievers, for them is a severe punishment.
versets.commentaire: “He increases for them of His favor”: Qushayrī reports the interpretation of the people of the Sunna, for whom this increase is the vision (al-ruʾya). And he distinguishes finely: acts of obedience have measured degrees facing them, rising or falling according to them; but the vision belongs to surplus and pure favor — and favor knows no gradation. Then he opens a door of immense hope to the sinner: even one who has no obedience worthy of acceptance possesses a repentance which, if he offers it, will be accepted.

mots.racine: ز ي د / ف ض ل
mots.sens: He increases for them of His favor
mots.def: per the Sufis: the surplus of the vision.

versets.ar: وَلَوْ بَسَطَ اللَّهُ الرِّزْقَ لِعِبادِهِ لَبَغَوْا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلكِنْ يُنَزِّلُ بِقَدَرٍ ما يَشاءُ إِنَّهُ بِعِبادِهِ خَبِيرٌ بَصِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-law basaṭa llāhu r-rizqa li-ʿibādihi la-baghaw fī l-arḍi wa-lākin yunazzilu bi-qadarin mā yashāʾu innahu bi-ʿibādihi khabīrun baṣīrun
versets.trad: Were God to extend provision lavishly to His servants, they would transgress on the earth; but He sends down in measure what He wills. He is well-informed of His servants, and sees them clearly.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī hears this verse as an intimate word of excuse, in the manner men excuse one another: “I did not widen the world for you, O poor one, because I knew that if I apportioned it to you lavishly, you would rebel and spread corruption on the earth.” And the “but” is a tender correction: I did not dilate your provision as much as you wish, yet I did not withhold it all from you — for I send down according to the measure I will. Poverty is thus neither abandonment nor punishment, but solicitude that knows the servant better than he knows himself.

mots.sens: they would transgress on the earth
mots.def: baghā: to transgress, to grow insolent.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / ق د ر

versets.ar: وَهُوَ الَّذِي يُنَزِّلُ الْغَيْثَ مِنْ بَعْدِ ما قَنَطُوا وَيَنْشُرُ رَحْمَتَهُ وَهُوَ الْوَلِيُّ الْحَمِيدُ
versets.tr: wa-huwa lladhī yunazzilu l-ghaytha min baʿdi mā qanaṭū wa-yanshuru raḥmatahu wa-huwa l-waliyyu l-ḥamīdu
versets.trad: It is He who sends down the rain after they have despaired, and spreads His mercy. He is the Protector, the Praiseworthy.
versets.commentaire: This is one of the most beautiful ishārāt of the sūra. God is the reviver of hearts: just as He sends down the rain after despair and spreads His mercy — when the land has dried up, the rain has tarried, the people have lost all hope and the moment verges on extinction — so too with the servant: when the branch of his moment has withered, when the limpidity of his love has clouded, when the sun of his intimacy has eclipsed and his covenant has drawn far from the courts of nearness, it may be that the Real looks upon him with His mercy and rains upon his secret the downpours of grace: his branch grows tender again, and in the places of his intimacy a freshly gathered rose blooms anew.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / غ ي ث

versets.ar: وَمِنْ آياتِهِ خَلْقُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَما بَثَّ فِيهِما مِنْ دابَّةٍ وَهُوَ عَلى جَمْعِهِمْ إِذا يَشاءُ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-min āyātihi khalqu s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-mā baththa fīhimā min dābbatin wa-huwa ʿalā jamʿihim idhā yashāʾu qadīrun
versets.trad: Among His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the living creatures He has scattered throughout them. He is able to gather them whenever He wills.
versets.commentaire: In every creature God has placed an indication of His oneness in His majesty. Then Qushayrī draws from “He is able to gather them” a delicate ishāra on the friends of God: the Real is jealous of His intimates: He fears that some should rest their hearts in others, so He ceaselessly scatters their gathering; rarely is an assembly of the people of the heart found together in one place, and for a short while. But sometimes He shows them grace: the dwellings draw near, an encounter occurs among them — for the Real has seen in their gathering blessings for the life of the world. And this, though rare, He is able to do when He wills.

mots.racine: ب ث ث / د ب ب
mots.sens: He scattered living creatures in them
mots.def: baththa: to disperse; dābba: a creature that moves.

mots.racine: ج م ع / ق د ر

versets.ar: وَما أَصابَكُمْ مِنْ مُصِيبَةٍ فَبِما كَسَبَتْ أَيْدِيكُمْ وَيَعْفُوا عَنْ كَثِيرٍ
versets.tr: wa-mā aṣābakum min muṣībatin fa-bi-mā kasabat aydīkum wa-yaʿfū ʿan kathīrin
versets.trad: Whatever misfortune befalls you is for what your own hands have earned; yet He pardons much.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse a school for the soul. When the servant realizes this verse, and a wound or a grievous state befalls him, knowing it to be the recompense of some lapse in beautiful conduct, he is ashamed of his act; this turns him away from looking at people: he seeks neither to avenge himself on them nor to curse them, but is absorbed in repairing the wrong escaped from him rather than in claiming his due from those who dominate him — leaving the whole affair to his Lord. And he adds: when trials accumulate and follow upon one another, let him reflect on his blameworthy acts: how many must he commit to reach — despite the abundant pardon — such a degree of recompense?

mots.racine: ع ف و / ك ث ر

versets.ar: وَما أَنْتُمْ بِمُعْجِزِينَ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَما لَكُمْ مِنْ دُونِ اللَّهِ مِنْ وَلِيٍّ وَلا نَصِيرٍ
versets.tr: wa-mā antum bi-muʿjizīna fī l-arḍi wa-mā lakum min dūni llāhi min waliyyin wa-lā naṣīrin
versets.trad: You cannot escape [His power] on the earth, and you have, apart from God, neither protector nor helper.
versets.commentaire: You cannot reduce God to impotence nor escape Him on the earth. In the sūra's wake, Qushayrī recalls that no ally nor helper is to be found apart from Him: the servant who thinks himself freed from His grasp only misjudges his own weakness. All true protection traces back to the sole Protector; all help that does not flow from Him is but a mirage. There is no refuge from God except in God Himself.

mots.def: aʿjaza: to make incapable, to escape.

mots.racine: و ل ي / ن ص ر

versets.ar: وَمِنْ آياتِهِ الْجَوارِ فِي الْبَحْرِ كَالْأَعْلامِ
versets.tr: wa-min āyātihi l-jawāri fī l-baḥri ka-l-aʿlāmi
versets.trad: Among His signs are the ships that sail the sea like mountains.
versets.commentaire: The ships that course the seas: God sends the wind that drives them, then stills it, and shows them by turns destruction or safety — to rouse them to constant reflection and vigilance. Qushayrī draws the ishāra: God holds people, during the interval of the moment, sheltered from the contrary squalls, and keeps them in the haven of safety. The duty is therefore gratitude in every state; and when gratitude is purified, it draws the abundant increase.

mots.def: al-jawārī: ships that run upon the water.

versets.ar: إِنْ يَشَأْ يُسْكِنِ الرِّيحَ فَيَظْلَلْنَ رَواكِدَ عَلى ظَهْرِهِ إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ لِكُلِّ صَبَّارٍ شَكُورٍ
versets.tr: in yashaʾ yuskini r-rīḥa fa-yaẓlalna rawākida ʿalā ẓahrihi inna fī dhālika la-āyātin li-kulli ṣabbārin shakūrin
versets.trad: If He wills, He stills the wind and they lie motionless on its surface — surely in that are signs for every steadfast, grateful one,
versets.commentaire: If He wills, He stills the wind and the ships lie motionless on the sea's back. Qushayrī notes that the sign is offered especially to the “much-patient, much-grateful” (ṣabbār shakūr): for the true servant joins endurance in trial to gratitude in blessing. The calm sea and the tempest are equally signs; but only he deciphers them who knows how to give thanks in rest and to be patient in the shaking.

mots.racine: س ك ن / ر و ح
mots.def: askana: to cause to cease; rīḥ: wind.

mots.racine: ص ب ر / ش ك ر

versets.ar: أَوْ يُوبِقْهُنَّ بِما كَسَبُوا وَيَعْفُ عَنْ كَثِيرٍ
versets.tr: aw yūbiqhunna bi-mā kasabū wa-yaʿfu ʿan kathīrin
versets.trad: or He wrecks them for what they have earned; yet He pardons much!
versets.commentaire: Or He makes them perish for what their hands have earned. But the verse does not close on chastisement: “and He pardons much.” Qushayrī, faithful to the spirit of the sūra, stresses that the balance always tips toward pardon: the wreck is deserved, but the grace that spares far exceeds the sanction that strikes. The “much” that is excused outweighs the little that is punished.

versets.ar: وَيَعْلَمَ الَّذِينَ يُجادِلُونَ فِي آياتِنا ما لَهُمْ مِنْ مَحِيصٍ
versets.tr: wa-yaʿlama lladhīna yujādilūna fī āyātinā mā lahum min maḥīṣin
versets.trad: And those who dispute about Our signs may know that they have no escape.
versets.commentaire: Let those who dispute about Our signs know that no refuge opens for them. In continuity with the sūra, Qushayrī sees in this absence of escape (maḥīṣ) the end of all vain polemic: the denier who thinks to flee through argument only tightens around himself the circle of his own impasse. Outside surrender to the Truth there is no passage.

versets.ar: فَما أُوتِيتُمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ فَمَتاعُ الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا وَما عِنْدَ اللَّهِ خَيْرٌ وَأَبْقى لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَلى رَبِّهِمْ يَتَوَكَّلُونَ
versets.tr: fa-mā ūtītum min shayʾin fa-matāʿu l-ḥayāti d-dunyā wa-mā ʿinda llāhi khayrun wa-abqā li-lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿalā rabbihim yatawakkalūna
versets.trad: Whatever you have been given is but the enjoyment of the life of this world. What is with God is better and more lasting, for those who believe and trust in their Lord,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī contrasts the quality of the two worlds with striking economy: the joys of this world are never limpid, never free of disturbances; and when one happens to taste of them at moments, they are quick to vanish, a viaticum swiftly struck. “And what is with God” — that is, the promised reward — is better than this little that is present. The lasting prevails over the perishable for whoever believes and entrusts his fate to his Lord.

mots.racine: م ت ع / ح ي ي
mots.sens: enjoyment of the life of this world

mots.racine: خ ي ر / ب ق ي

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ يَجْتَنِبُونَ كَبائِرَ الْإِثْمِ وَالْفَواحِشَ وَإِذا ما غَضِبُوا هُمْ يَغْفِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna yajtanibūna kabāʾira l-ithmi wa-l-fawāḥisha wa-idhā mā ghaḍibū hum yaghfirūna
versets.trad: those who shun the grave sins and indecencies, and who forgive even when they are angry,
versets.commentaire: “The grave sins” (kabāʾir al-ithm), Qushayrī specifies, designate associating partners; “the indecencies” (fawāḥish), the lapses below that. When they abstain from them, they no longer swallow the cups of anger; rather, the seething of the soul subsides in them, for they entrust themselves to their Lord in all their states. Forgiveness offered in the very midst of anger is thus no weakness but the summit of trust: the soul handed over to God no longer demands its due.

mots.racine: ك ب ر / أ ث م
mots.def: kabāʾir: major sins; per Qushayrī, associating partners.

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ اسْتَجابُوا لِرَبِّهِمْ وَأَقامُوا الصَّلاةَ وَأَمْرُهُمْ شُورى بَيْنَهُمْ وَمِمَّا رَزَقْناهُمْ يُنْفِقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna stajābū li-rabbihim wa-aqāmū ṣ-ṣalāta wa-amruhum shūrā baynahum wa-mimmā razaqnāhum yunfiqūna
versets.trad: those who answer their Lord, establish the prayer, whose affair is consultation among themselves, and who spend from what We have provided them,
versets.commentaire: Here is the verse that gives the sūra its name. Qushayrī defines the true answering of God: he who answers his Lord is one for whom not a breath remains that does not accord with His good pleasure, and for whom no portion of self remains for himself. As for “their affair is consultation among them”: none of them decides alone by his own opinion, for he always holds his own judgment and view in suspicion; then, when he wishes to conclude a matter, he entrusts it to God. Shūrā is thus a humility of the heart before being a rule of governance: to distrust oneself in order to rely on Him.

mots.sens: their affair is consultation among them

mots.racine: ر ز ق / ن ف ق
mots.sens: they spend from what We provided them

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ إِذا أَصابَهُمُ الْبَغْيُ هُمْ يَنْتَصِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna idhā aṣābahumu l-baghyu hum yantaṣirūna
versets.trad: and those who, when wrong is done to them, defend themselves.
versets.commentaire: “Aggression” (al-baghy) is injustice. But Qushayrī offers an unexpected inner reading: each of them knows that the injustice that strikes him comes from his own soul; so he triumphs over the oppressor — which is none other than his soul — by curbing its reins, preventing it from galloping in the field of transgressions. True “defending” is thus first not against others, but against the soul that incites to evil.

mots.racine: ص و ب / ب غ ي

mots.def: intaṣara: to take redress, to triumph.

versets.ar: وَجَزاءُ سَيِّئَةٍ سَيِّئَةٌ مِثْلُها فَمَنْ عَفا وَأَصْلَحَ فَأَجْرُهُ عَلَى اللَّهِ إِنَّهُ لا يُحِبُّ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-jazāʾu sayyiʾatin sayyiʾatun mithluhā fa-man ʿafā wa-aṣlaḥa fa-ajruhu ʿalā llāhi innahu lā yuḥibbu ẓ-ẓālimīna
versets.trad: The recompense of an evil is an evil like it; but whoever pardons and reconciles, his reward is with God. Indeed, He loves not the wrongdoers.
versets.commentaire: “Do not exceed, in retaliation or vengeance, the measure of the wrong the aggressor did to you”: such is the law of equity. But Qushayrī dwells on the summit: whoever pardons the aggressor and repairs what is between him and God, God repairs what is between him and people. And he unveils the sense of “his reward is incumbent on God”: what the servant receives from God, through God and with God, is better than all he would have gained by his own choosing. To renounce one's due is to entrust it to the most generous of reckoners.

mots.racine: ج ز ي / س و أ
mots.sens: the recompense of an evil is an evil like it

versets.ar: وَلَمَنِ انْتَصَرَ بَعْدَ ظُلْمِهِ فَأُولئِكَ ما عَلَيْهِمْ مِنْ سَبِيلٍ
versets.tr: wa-la-mani ntaṣara baʿda ẓulmihi fa-ulāʾika mā ʿalayhim min sabīlin
versets.trad: And whoever defends himself after being wronged — against such there is no blame.
versets.commentaire: Whoever defends himself after being wronged incurs no blame. Qushayrī observes that God knows the measure of His servants: He knows that not all could free themselves from the soul's demands nor establish themselves in beauty of character; so He has granted them retaliation in the mode of justice and equity — though the most excellent for them was pardon and indulgence. The right to reply is a permission, not an ideal: the high road remains that of pardon.

mots.racine: ن ص ر / ظ ل م
mots.sens: defends himself after the wrong suffered
no way of reproach against them

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا السَّبِيلُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ يَظْلِمُونَ النَّاسَ وَيَبْغُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ بِغَيْرِ الْحَقِّ أُولئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: innamā s-sabīlu ʿalā lladhīna yaẓlimūna n-nāsa wa-yabghūna fī l-arḍi bi-ghayri l-ḥaqqi ulāʾika lahum ʿadhābun alīmun
versets.trad: Blame lies only against those who wrong people and transgress on the earth without right. For them is a painful punishment.
versets.commentaire: Blame weighs only on the oppressors. Qushayrī specifies: the way of censure is open against him who has exceeded the limit, overstepped the measure and committed what was not permitted him — for such is a painful punishment. Justice does not pursue the one who defends himself, but the one who initiates the wrong and sows violence without right on the earth. The division is clear: the wronged is acquitted, the aggressor is given over to the penalty.

mots.def: baghā: to oppress, to sow violence.

versets.ar: وَلَمَنْ صَبَرَ وَغَفَرَ إِنَّ ذلِكَ لَمِنْ عَزْمِ الْأُمُورِ
versets.tr: wa-la-man ṣabara wa-ghafara inna dhālika la-min ʿazmi l-umūri
versets.trad: But whoever endures and forgives — that is indeed among the matters requiring firm resolve.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives here a luminous definition of accomplished patience: to endure the trial without complaint, to forgive — by overlooking the adversary — without keeping against him any claim, but absolving him of every demand, here below as in the hereafter: that is what belongs to the resolute firmness of affairs (ʿazm al-umūr). Full forgiveness does not only efface the other's debt: it frees the heart of all litigation, in this world and the next.

mots.racine: ص ب ر / غ ف ر
ع ز م / أ م ر

versets.ar: وَمَنْ يُضْلِلِ اللَّهُ فَما لَهُ مِنْ وَلِيٍّ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ وَتَرَى الظَّالِمِينَ لَمَّا رَأَوُا الْعَذابَ يَقُولُونَ هَلْ إِلى مَرَدٍّ مِنْ سَبِيلٍ
versets.tr: wa-man yuḍlili llāhu fa-mā lahu min waliyyin min baʿdihi wa-tarā ẓ-ẓālimīna lammā raʾawu l-ʿadhāba yaqūlūna hal ilā maraddin min sabīlin
versets.trad: Whomever God sends astray has no protector after Him. You will see the wrongdoers, when they behold the punishment, saying: “Is there any way to return?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī describes the lot of the astray: those whom God has sent astray, whose outer and inner sight He has blinded, whom He has cast into the toil of their chastisement and deprived of the coolness of contentment with their Lord's decree, have no protector apart from God, no shield against His punishment. And when they see the torment, they beg for deliverance without obtaining it: “Is there any way to return?” — the question of regret when the door of return has closed.

mots.racine: ر د د / س ب ل
mots.sens: is there any way to return?

versets.ar: وَتَراهُمْ يُعْرَضُونَ عَلَيْها خاشِعِينَ مِنَ الذُّلِّ يَنْظُرُونَ مِنْ طَرْفٍ خَفِيٍّ وَقالَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنَّ الْخاسِرِينَ الَّذِينَ خَسِرُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ وَأَهْلِيهِمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ أَلا إِنَّ الظَّالِمِينَ فِي عَذابٍ مُقِيمٍ
versets.tr: wa-tarāhum yuʿraḍūna ʿalayhā khāshiʿīna mina dh-dhulli yanẓurūna min ṭarfin khafiyyin wa-qāla lladhīna āmanū inna l-khāsirīna lladhīna khasirū anfusahum wa-ahlīhim yawma l-qiyāmati alā inna ẓ-ẓālimīna fī ʿadhābin muqīmin
versets.trad: You will see them brought before it, bowed down in humiliation, looking with stealthy glances. Those who believed will say: “Truly the losers are those who have lost themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection.” Behold, the wrongdoers are in a lasting torment!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī paints the scene with severe compassion: you see them exposed to the Fire, bowed in humiliation; no remorse avails them now, no call is heard from them; the believers remind them of what they had warned them, but they do not hear. That Day, no helper to help them, no merciful one to show them mercy. The “stealthy glance” tells the shame of one who no longer dares raise his eyes; and “those who lost themselves” designates the most radical loss: not of a good, but of the self.

mots.racine: خ ش ع / ذ ل ل
خ س ر / ن ف س
mots.def: khasira: to lose; nafs: soul, self.

versets.ar: وَما كانَ لَهُمْ مِنْ أَوْلِياءَ يَنْصُرُونَهُمْ مِنْ دُونِ اللَّهِ وَمَنْ يُضْلِلِ اللَّهُ فَما لَهُ مِنْ سَبِيلٍ
versets.tr: wa-mā kāna lahum min awliyāʾa yanṣurūnahum min dūni llāhi wa-man yuḍlili llāhu fa-mā lahu min sabīlin
versets.trad: They will have no protectors to help them apart from God. Whomever God sends astray has no way.
versets.commentaire: A solemn restatement of the theme: no ally apart from God can help them, and whomever God sends astray finds no way. Qushayrī inscribes this verse in the sūra's seal upon false protectors: those who had taken as allies other than God discover, on the Day of judgment, that they are without any support. The path (sabīl) that closes is that of return to God, once open and neglected.

mots.sens: to help them apart from God

versets.ar: اسْتَجِيبُوا لِرَبِّكُمْ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يَأْتِيَ يَوْمٌ لا مَرَدَّ لَهُ مِنَ اللَّهِ ما لَكُمْ مِنْ مَلْجَإٍ يَوْمَئِذٍ وَما لَكُمْ مِنْ نَكِيرٍ
versets.tr: istajībū li-rabbikum min qabli an yaʾtiya yawmun lā maradda lahu mina llāhi mā lakum min maljaʾin yawmaʾidhin wa-mā lakum min nakīrin
versets.trad: Answer your Lord before there comes a Day that God will not turn back. You will have, that Day, no refuge and no denial.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of the call to answer a definition of fidelity: to answer God is to keep His covenant, to fulfill His right, to turn back from disobeying Him to obeying Him, and to surrender at every moment to His decree. Then he presses, with overwhelming urgency: the way of answering is open today; but soon the door will suddenly close upon the heart, and one will be seized unawares. The “before there comes a Day” is the most pressing warning: the present moment is the sole window of return.

mots.def: istajāba: to respond to the call.

mots.sens: that none can turn back, from God

versets.ar: فَإِنْ أَعْرَضُوا فَما أَرْسَلْناكَ عَلَيْهِمْ حَفِيظاً إِنْ عَلَيْكَ إِلاَّ الْبَلاغُ وَإِنَّا إِذا أَذَقْنَا الْإِنْسانَ مِنَّا رَحْمَةً فَرِحَ بِها وَإِنْ تُصِبْهُمْ سَيِّئَةٌ بِما قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ فَإِنَّ الْإِنْسانَ كَفُورٌ
versets.tr: fa-in aʿraḍū fa-mā arsalnāka ʿalayhim ḥafīẓan in ʿalayka illā l-balāghu wa-innā idhā adhaqnā l-insāna minnā raḥmatan fariḥa bihā wa-in tuṣibhum sayyiʾatun bi-mā qaddamat aydīhim fa-inna l-insāna kafūrun
versets.trad: If they turn away, We have not sent you as a guardian over them. Upon you is only the conveying of the message. When We let man taste a mercy from Us, he rejoices in it; but if an evil befalls him for what his hands have done, then man is ungrateful!
versets.commentaire: If they turn away, your charge is only the conveying; the rest, We know better how to deal with them. Then Qushayrī unmasks the fickleness of the human heart: when We let man taste ease and blessing, he rejoices in that state, but meets it with arrogance; he uses his full health to disobey, and makes safety a pretext for rebellion. And should a trial, a calamity or a misfortune touch him, behold him ungrateful for Our blessings, a denier of Our signs. Prosperity puffs him up, trial embitters him: such is the soul as long as it has not surrendered to God.

versets.ar: لِلَّهِ مُلْكُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ يَخْلُقُ ما يَشاءُ يَهَبُ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ إِناثاً وَيَهَبُ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ الذُّكُورَ
versets.tr: li-llāhi mulku s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi yakhluqu mā yashāʾu yahabu li-man yashāʾu ināthan wa-yahabu li-man yashāʾu dh-dhukūra
versets.trad: To God belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth. He creates what He wills. He bestows females upon whom He wills, and bestows males upon whom He wills.
themes: the gift according to His will
versets.commentaire: To God belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth: He creates what He wills. Qushayrī stresses the pure sovereignty of the gift: He grants daughters to whom He wills, sons to whom He wills, by His will alone. No claim stands before His decree, no human preference constrains Him. The gift of offspring, like every gift, belongs to a free will that none can bend or judge.

mots.sens: the dominion of the heavens and the earth
He bestows upon whom He wills

versets.ar: أَوْ يُزَوِّجُهُمْ ذُكْراناً وَإِناثاً وَيَجْعَلُ مَنْ يَشاءُ عَقِيماً إِنَّهُ عَلِيمٌ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: aw yuzawwijuhum dhukrānan wa-ināthan wa-yajʿalu man yashāʾu ʿaqīman innahu ʿalīmun qadīrun
versets.trad: Or He couples them, males and females, and makes whom He wills barren. Indeed, He is Knowing, Powerful.
versets.commentaire: He pairs males and females, or makes barren whom He wills. Qushayrī seals the teaching with the affirmation of sovereignty without recourse: no objection against His decree, no encroachment on His choice: He has more right over His servants than they have over themselves. Barrenness itself is not a lack but a free disposition of Him who is Knowing (ʿalīm) and Powerful (qadīr): He knows what He does and can do all He wills.

mots.sens: He couples them, males and females
He makes barren whom He wills

versets.ar: وَما كانَ لِبَشَرٍ أَنْ يُكَلِّمَهُ اللَّهُ إِلاَّ وَحْياً أَوْ مِنْ وَراءِ حِجابٍ أَوْ يُرْسِلَ رَسُولاً فَيُوحِيَ بِإِذْنِهِ ما يَشاءُ إِنَّهُ عَلِيٌّ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-mā kāna li-basharin an yukallimahu llāhu illā waḥyan aw min warāʾi ḥijābin aw yursila rasūlan fa-yūḥiya bi-idhnihi mā yashāʾu innahu ʿaliyyun ḥakīmun
versets.trad: It is not granted to any mortal that God should speak to him save by inspiration, or from behind a veil, or by sending a messenger to whom is revealed, by His permission, what He wills. He is Sublime, Wise.
themes: the veil on the servant's side
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī here corrects a capital misapprehension about the “veil.” “From behind a veil”: that is, without the servant seeing the Real. The veiled one is the servant, not the Lord; and the veil consists in God's creating, in the place of vision, the impediment to seeing. Far exalted be God above being Himself behind a veil — for that is the attribute of delimited bodies over which a curtain is drawn! God speaks by direct inspiration, or from behind a veil, or by sending a messenger: three modes, but the veil never affects Him who speaks, only the one who hears. He is Sublime in His rank, Wise in His acts.

mots.def: ḥijāb: veil, on the servant's side alone.

versets.ar: وَكَذلِكَ أَوْحَيْنا إِلَيْكَ رُوحاً مِنْ أَمْرِنا ما كُنْتَ تَدْرِي مَا الْكِتابُ وَلا الْإِيمانُ وَلكِنْ جَعَلْناهُ نُوراً نَهْدِي بِهِ مَنْ نَشاءُ مِنْ عِبادِنا وَإِنَّكَ لَتَهْدِي إِلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ
versets.tr: wa-kadhālika awḥaynā ilayka rūḥan min amrinā mā kunta tadrī mā l-kitābu wa-lā l-īmānu wa-lākin jaʿalnāhu nūran nahdī bihi man nashāʾu min ʿibādinā wa-innaka la-tahdī ilā ṣirāṭin mustaqīmin
versets.trad: Thus have We revealed to you a Spirit from Our command. You did not know what the Book was, nor faith. But We made it a light by which We guide whom We will among Our servants. And you indeed guide to a straight path:
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī explains why the Qurʾān is named “Spirit” (rūḥ): because whoever believes in it sees his heart become alive through it. The Book is spirit as the soul is the life of the body: it animates the dead heart. “You did not know what the Book was, nor faith” — that is, the detail of the Laws and prescriptions, before revelation taught it to you. But We made it a light by which We guide whom We will among Our believing servants. The Qurʾān is thus at once a spirit that gives life and a light that orients.

mots.racine: ر و ح / أ م ر
mots.def: rūḥ: spirit; the Qurʾān that makes the heart live.

mots.racine: ج ع ل / ن و ر

versets.ar: صِراطِ اللَّهِ الَّذِي لَهُ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ أَلا إِلَى اللَّهِ تَصِيرُ الْأُمُورُ
versets.tr: ṣirāṭi llāhi lladhī lahu mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi alā ilā llāhi taṣīru l-umūru
versets.trad: the path of God, to whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Behold, to God do all things return.
versets.commentaire: The sūra seals on the Path of God — that to which the Prophet guides — and on the universal return. Qushayrī illumines the end by the beginning: “is it not to God that all affairs return? — for from Him is their beginning.” Everything departs from Him and everything returns to Him: the circle of existence opens and closes upon the same Face. To Him belongs what is in the heavens and on the earth; and toward Him, the term of every thing.

mots.racine: ص ي ر / أ م ر
mots.sens: to God do all things return
mots.def: ṣāra: to become, to end up; umūr: affairs, things.
